description: Bluetooth proximity-based screen lock for Linux — next generation features

tasks.title: Relay Attack Detection
tasks.description: Detect BLE relay attacks by monitoring for physically impossible RSSI jumps (e.g., device teleports from OUT_OF_RANGE to IMMEDIATE in one reading). Flag sudden RSSI changes exceeding 20dBm between consecutive readings. Also detect unnaturally stable RSSI patterns typical of relayed signals by measuring RSSI variance over a sliding window.
acceptance_criteria: Detect RSSI jumps > 20dBm between consecutive readings and log a warning
Calculate RSSI variance over a 10-reading sliding window
Flag variance < 0.5 dBm as suspicious (relayed signal)
Add relay_attack_detection = true config option under [security]
Add configurable rssi_jump_threshold and min_rssi_variance
Unit tests covering jump detection, variance detection, and config parsing
No false positives during normal walk-away/return scenarios

tasks.title: Require User Activity on Unlock
tasks.description: Optional soft second factor: after Bluetooth proximity triggers unlock, require keyboard or mouse activity within N seconds. If no activity is detected, re-lock the screen. This prevents relay attacks from silently unlocking an unattended machine.
acceptance_criteria: Monitor X11/Wayland input events after unlock trigger
If no keyboard/mouse activity within configurable timeout (default 30s), re-lock
Add unlock_activity_timeout config option under [security]
Can be disabled by setting timeout to 0
Desktop notification warns user: 'Move mouse or press a key to confirm unlock'
Unit tests for timeout logic

tasks.title: Adapter Failure Auto-Recovery
tasks.description: Detect when the Bluetooth adapter crashes, is removed, or stops responding. Lock the screen immediately (secure default), notify the user, and attempt to re-initialize when the adapter returns.
acceptance_criteria: Detect adapter power-off or removal via D-Bus signal monitoring
Lock screen immediately when adapter fails (secure default)
Notify user: 'Bluetooth adapter lost — screen locked for security'
Periodically attempt to reconnect (every 10s, configurable)
Resume normal operation when adapter returns
Add adapter_recovery_interval to [timing] config
Unit tests for adapter failure and recovery state transitions

tasks.title: TX Power Distance Estimation
tasks.description: Use the BLE advertised TX Power to calculate actual distance instead of raw RSSI. Formula: distance = 10^((txPower - rssi) / (10 * n)) where n is the path-loss exponent. Read TX Power from BlueZ device properties when available, fall back to rssi_at_1m calibration value.
acceptance_criteria: Read TxPower property from BlueZ D-Bus device interface
Calculate distance using log-distance path loss model
Add path_loss_exponent config option per device (default 2.0)
Use calculated distance alongside RSSI for zone classification
Fall back to pure RSSI when TxPower is not available
Log estimated distance in verbose mode
Unit tests for distance calculation with various inputs

tasks.title: Interactive RSSI Calibration Wizard
tasks.description: Add a 'blueward calibrate' command that guides the user through measuring RSSI at known distances (1m, 3m, 5m). Record samples, compute averages, and auto-configure zone thresholds and rssi_at_1m for their specific device and environment.
acceptance_criteria: New 'blueward calibrate' CLI subcommand
Prompts user: 'Hold phone at 1 meter... press Enter when ready'
Collects 20 RSSI samples at each distance (1m, 3m, 5m)
Computes mean and standard deviation for each distance
Auto-calculates zone thresholds from measured data
Writes calibrated values to config.toml (with backup)
Shows calibration results summary
Handles case where device is not detected during calibration

tasks.title: Tray Icon Snooze / Temporary Disable
tasks.description: Add a 'Snooze' submenu to the system tray with options: 15 minutes, 30 minutes, 1 hour, Until I resume. During snooze, BlueWard suspends proximity monitoring and shows a snooze indicator. Essential for presentations or when intentionally leaving phone elsewhere.
acceptance_criteria: Snooze submenu in tray with 15m, 30m, 1h, and 'Until resume' options
Timer-based auto-resume after snooze period
Tray icon changes to indicate snooze state
Status label shows 'Snoozed (Xm remaining)'
Desktop notification when snooze starts and ends
Snooze state survives tray icon rebuild but not service restart
Unit tests for snooze timer logic

tasks.title: Zone-Triggered Custom Actions
tasks.description: Allow users to configure custom commands for each zone transition, not just lock/unlock. For example: dim screen when entering FAR zone, pause media when leaving NEAR, run arbitrary scripts per zone.
acceptance_criteria: New [actions.zones] config section with on_immediate, on_near, on_far, on_out_of_range
Each zone action is a shell command string
Actions fire on zone transitions (not continuously)
Previous zone's exit action fires before new zone's enter action
Actions are optional — empty string means no action
Log each action execution in verbose mode
Unit tests for zone action dispatch

tasks.title: Graduated Warnings Before Lock
tasks.description: Instead of a single notification then lock, implement escalating warnings: (1) subtle notification when entering FAR zone, (2) screen dim when grace period starts, (3) lock when grace period expires. Give the user visual feedback and time to return.
acceptance_criteria: Notification when device enters FAR zone: 'Device getting far'
Optional screen dimming during DEVICE_LEAVING grace period
Restore brightness if device returns during grace period
Add graduated_warnings = true config option
Add dim_percentage = 50 config option (how much to dim)
Works on GNOME, KDE (via D-Bus brightness API)
Falls back gracefully if brightness control is unavailable

tasks.title: Event History and Audit Log
tasks.description: Maintain a structured JSON log of all lock/unlock events with timestamps, device info, RSSI at time of event, and trigger reason. Viewable from tray icon menu and useful for debugging and security auditing.
acceptance_criteria: JSON Lines log file at ~/.local/share/blueward/events.jsonl
Each event: timestamp, event_type, device_name, device_mac, rssi, zone, trigger
Event types: lock, unlock, snooze, resume, adapter_error, adapter_recovered
Tray menu item: 'Recent Events' showing last 10 events
Log rotation: keep last 10,000 events (configurable)
Add event_log = true and event_log_max_entries config options
Unit tests for event logging and rotation

tasks.title: D-Bus API
tasks.description: Expose BlueWard's state over D-Bus (org.blueward.Service) so other applications, scripts, and desktop environments can query proximity status, subscribe to state changes, and control the service.
acceptance_criteria: D-Bus service at org.blueward.Service on session bus
Properties: State, DeviceZone, DeviceRssi, IsPaused, IsSnoozing
Methods: Pause(), Resume(), Snooze(minutes), GetDeviceInfo()
Signals: StateChanged(old, new), DeviceZoneChanged(mac, zone)
Introspectable interface for discovery
Unit tests with mocked D-Bus

tasks.title: Smart Media Control
tasks.description: Built-in playerctl integration that intelligently pauses media on lock and resumes on unlock. Only pause if something is actually playing, only resume what was paused by BlueWard (not manually paused media).
acceptance_criteria: Detect if media is currently playing via playerctl before pausing
Track which players were paused by BlueWard
Only resume players that BlueWard paused
Add media_control = true config option
Works with Spotify, Firefox, VLC, and any MPRIS-compatible player
Graceful fallback if playerctl is not installed
Unit tests for pause/resume tracking logic

tasks.title: Device Battery Level Display
tasks.description: Read the trusted device's battery level from BLE GATT Battery Service (0x180F) or BlueZ cached properties. Display in tray icon tooltip and warn when battery is low (dead phone = unexpected lockout).
acceptance_criteria: Read battery level from BlueZ Device1.Battery property or GATT
Display battery percentage in tray tooltip
Low battery notification when below 20% (configurable threshold)
Critical battery notification when below 10%
Tray menu shows: 'Device: My Phone (72%)'
Graceful handling when battery info is unavailable
Add battery_warning_threshold config option

tasks.title: Wayland Compositor Support
tasks.description: Ensure lock/unlock and screen brightness control work on Wayland compositors: GNOME Wayland, KDE Plasma Wayland, Sway, Hyprland. Some D-Bus interfaces differ between X11 and Wayland.
acceptance_criteria: Lock works on: GNOME Wayland, KDE Wayland, Sway (swaylock), Hyprland (hyprlock)
Unlock works or gracefully degrades on each
Auto-detect compositor and use appropriate lock command
Screen brightness control works on Wayland
Document any compositor-specific limitations
Add wayland_lock_command config override

tasks.title: Systemd Watchdog Integration
tasks.description: Integrate with systemd's watchdog mechanism (WatchdogSec) to auto-restart BlueWard if it hangs. The service periodically notifies systemd it's alive; if it stops, systemd restarts it.
acceptance_criteria: Send sd_notify WATCHDOG=1 every N seconds from the main loop
Update blueward.service with WatchdogSec=30
Lock screen on unclean restart (WATCHDOG_USEC timeout)
Log watchdog notifications in debug mode
Works without systemd (graceful no-op)
